questions.question: Directions: Read the following statements, then select your answer from alternatives A-D below. Statement 1: Unless an emergency is in progress on the property, firefighters cannot enter private property without being invited by the owner or occupant. Statement 2: Under common and most statute law, the existence of a fire or other emergency constitutes implied permission to enter. Statement 3: The local governing body must adopt an ordinance that authorizes the fire department to enter private property to conduct fire and life safety inspections.

choices.text: Statement 1 is true; statements 2 and 3 are false.
Statement 1 is false; statements 2 and 3 are true.
Statements 1 and 2 are false; statement 3 is true.

questions.question: Adequate preplanning of structures will:

choices.text: provide crews with additional work not related to their job description.

questions.question: While speaking to a group of business owners about the importance of preplanning, a cellular phone rings. This situation would be:
The fire department should establish a written policy that covers:

choices.text: terminating the employee for any offense.
suspension with pay to investigate the incident.
referrals to the Employee Assistance Program.

questions.correctChoiceText: referrals to the Employee Assistance Program.

questions.question: The Incident Commander's understanding of fire growth and development will assist in determining:
At an emergency scene, the function served by the Operations section is:

choices.text: controlling and overseeing equipment and personnel inventories.
monitoring the incident and personnel, assuring compliance with safety procedures and limiting access to the hot and warm zones of the incident.
coordinating all medical and health concerns at the incident.
overseeing the tactical objectives to meet the strategic goals developed by Command.

questions.correctChoiceText: overseeing the tactical objectives to meet the strategic goals developed by Command.

questions.question: Which is a Company Officer I responsibility after a fire?
Feedback is:

choices.text: a person who receives the message.
an individual who initiates a message.
a method for verifying a message.
a process for exchanging information and attitudes.

questions.correctChoiceText: a method for verifying a message.

questions.question: Single resource is:

choices.text: a set number of resources of the same kind and type.
a specified number of personnel assembled for an assignment.
any combination of resources assembled for a specific mission.
individual pieces of apparatus and the personnel required to make them functional.

questions.correctChoiceText: individual pieces of apparatus and the personnel required to make them functional.

questions.question: Policies should be put in writing to:

choices.text: remove personnel from the requirement to make judgments.
serve as a source of reference for personnel hiring questions.
make the intent of management ambiguous.

questions.question: To improve success in delegation, you should:

choices.text: reserve feedback until the assignment is complete.
provide step-by step actions to be followed.
give authority to match the level of responsibility.

questions.correctChoiceText: give authority to match the level of responsibility.

questions.question: The three major tasks with any pre-incident plan involve gathering the data, entering the data into databases, and:

choices.text: revising codes based on the survey outcomes.

questions.question: The lesson plan should identify the:

choices.text: definition of unsafe acts and conditions.
disciplinary action for failure to perform.

questions.question: Which applies to written communications?

choices.text: The number of paragraphs depends on the number of subdivisions in the outline.
Typing paragraphs with transitional ideas does little to flow or rhythm of the document.
The topic sentence must always occur at the beginning of the document.
The average paragraph is 45 words long.

questions.correctChoiceText: The number of paragraphs depends on the number of subdivisions in the outline.

questions.question: The process of gathering and evaluating information about a particular building or occupancy is:
Which statement is true with regard to the four-step method of instruction?

choices.text: Most learning takes place during the presentation step.
Company officers should not alter the four-step method of instruction.
Lesson plans are generally developed by the company officer.
Company officers should not alter the lesson plan to meet the existing conditions.

questions.correctChoiceText: Company officers should not alter the four-step method of instruction.

questions.question: The strategic type plan:

choices.text: is usually intended to reach an objective within a short period of time.
attempts to take into account the external factors that will affect the organization.
attempts to prepare for the worst possible situations.
is concerned with those factors that are within the control of the organization.

questions.correctChoiceText: attempts to take into account the external factors that will affect the organization.

questions.question: The manner a promotion to company officer will affect you is that it:

choices.text: provides a method to share their legitimate authority with the crew.
can change the dynamics of the group.
allows the company officer the flexibility to act as one of the crew.
decreases the level of ethical responsibility.

questions.correctChoiceText: can change the dynamics of the group.

questions.question: The level of performance required for a specific job, as established by National Fire Protection Association professional qualifications, are:
An effective leader will resolve conflicts among subordinates by:

choices.text: imposing an arbitrary solution to the problem.

questions.question: The ability of a system to work with and use the parts or equipment of another system is:
The term message is:

choices.text: a process for exchanging information and attitudes.
the idea or item to be communicated.

questions.correctChoiceText: the idea or item to be communicated.

questions.question: A mission statement allows personnel to know:

choices.text: the basic reason for the organization and how it sees itself.
what will happen if they do not comply with policies.

questions.correctChoiceText: the basic reason for the organization and how it sees itself.

questions.question: Effective oral communication includes:

choices.text: not stopping during the speaking process since such pauses are distracting.
using words that accurately symbolize the image being conveyed.
speaking quickly so that the listener does not lose interest.
not looking at the person being addressed if discipline is involved.

questions.correctChoiceText: using words that accurately symbolize the image being conveyed.

questions.question: Delegation can:

choices.text: be taken too seriously by subordinates.
develop firefighters by expanding their capabilities.
give the company officer more time to perform technical skills.
be an effective tool to limit responsibility.

questions.correctChoiceText: develop firefighters by expanding their capabilities.

questions.question: Three main categories of record keeping at the company level are ___ records.
After an exposure has occurred, the department infection control officer should be notified:
To meet reasonable organizational expectations, you should use:
Setting the limits for expected performance and enforcing them is:
If there is an exposure, the person who would arrange for the necessary testing is the:
The development model step that involves conflict due to the informal leadership over the group is:
An officer's principal job responsibility is:
Discipline within a fire company is to:
A firefighter is frequently late for work caused by personal problems. You should:
You should understand that different cultures have different expectations such as:

choices.text: the concept of personal space varying within different cultural groups.
interpretations in various languages are similar enough to get the point across.
maintenance of eye contact is regarded as necessary in all cultures.
the second most common language in the United States is French.

questions.correctChoiceText: the concept of personal space varying within different cultural groups.

questions.question: Instructions: Read the following statements and select the correct answer from choices A-D below. Statement 1: Risk Watch is an all hazards program developed by the National Fire Academy for use in high schools. Statement 2: A risk reduction program such as bicycle helmet safety is considered to be a proactive approach to community involvement. Statement 3: Demographic data can be used to develop a public education program.

choices.text: Statement 1 is false; statements 2 and 3 are true.
Statement 1 is true; statements 2 and 3 are false.
Statements 1 and 2 are true; statement 3 is false.

questions.correctChoiceText: Statement 1 is false; statements 2 and 3 are true.

questions.question: The radio transmission "Ladder 65, from Operations--ventilate the roof" is a:
The length of time that sensitive files are active or archived is part of the:
The first step in developing a budget is to:

choices.text: plan your needs and projected costs.
find out what money is available.
submit a cost estimate to the city record for a cost breakdown.
call other agencies and use their budget figures.

questions.correctChoiceText: plan your needs and projected costs.

questions.question: Ventilation assignments would be considered a ___ activity.
Which document provides information on the actions taken to mitigate the incident?
In the ___ step budget development, chief officers meet with personnel to review the budget and explain what it means to the organization's operation.
Layman's sequence for identifying priorities and developing an action plan starts with:
A system of values or a standard of conduct that serves as a guideline for making decisions is:
Failure to know and apply the applicable laws and regulations could result in the company officer.

choices.text: being prosecuted for a civil violation.

questions.question: Building construction classifications are based on:

choices.text: materials used in construction and hourly fire resistive ratings.
each state's building department selects their own classifications.

questions.correctChoiceText: materials used in construction and hourly fire resistive ratings.

questions.question: When monitoring the evolution of a new skill, you notice an incorrect performance and you should:

choices.text: immediately stop and correct the performance.
speak with the student later as to not embarrass them.
allow the student to realize the problem.
allow the student continue until finished.

questions.correctChoiceText: immediately stop and correct the performance.

questions.question: If a citizen approaches you with an unusual request, what must be done?

choices.text: Listen and respond within your authority.
Refer the individual to the secretary.
Ignore the request because it is not fire related.
Seek immediate assistance from the chief.

questions.correctChoiceText: Listen and respond within your authority.

questions.question: The first step in preparing a narrative report is the:
Once initial size-up has been completed, the incident commander should:

choices.text: continue size-up and revise plans as conditions change.
follow the plan precisely, once it has been established.
have a charged line ready for use at the point of entry.
pass command to the company officer.

questions.correctChoiceText: continue size-up and revise plans as conditions change.

questions.question: Multiple agencies or jurisdictions that share in the decision-making process are operating under:
Drawings used to show the number of floors in a building and the grade of the surrounding ground is:
What is one of the disadvantages to passive information-gathering?
Data that describes the characteristics of a community and its population segments is:
A(n) ___ budget details the major equipment or building purchases the department plans to make.
Who has the ultimate responsibility for a fire department occupational safety and health program?
Records relating to fire pumps that are tested annually are:
Flammable waste, such as oily rags, must be kept in:
A company officer who allows sexually explicit pictures and comments that inhibit an employee's performance is:

choices.text: contributing to a hostile work environment.
protected from liability under sovereign immunity.
an example of a person with high power needs.

questions.correctChoiceText: contributing to a hostile work environment.

questions.question: During the initial report, you should decide:

choices.text: what component of the Incident Action plan will be initially completed.
what unit will be the Incident Commander.
who will be the first unit to demobilize.

questions.question: Floor plans drawn during building surveys should:

choices.text: show a cross section of construction features.
include the layout of individual floors.

questions.correctChoiceText: include the layout of individual floors.

questions.question: Locations that have great risk for loss of life or property are:
A fire department's capital budget:

choices.text: sets performance standards for each program area.
contains funds for equipment and building maintenance.
divides money into several accounts for day-to-day operations.

questions.question: You should be able to explain that the sexual harassment policy:

choices.text: is a protected right under the provision of affirmative action.
involves unwanted and unwelcome sexual behavior.
is not enforced as long as the contact is consensual.
is considered a reasonable accommodation under Title 7.

questions.correctChoiceText: involves unwanted and unwelcome sexual behavior.

questions.question: The act of directing, ordering, and controlling resources is:
Discipline is often thought of as:
The storage magazine required for black powder and smokeless powder is a Type ___ magazine.
What is accomplished in the identification step of public education program development?

choices.text: Scheduling and training of fire and life safety educators
Determining the time and place for delivering the message
Determining the cost and benefits of the new program

questions.question: Which type of building construction is protected by encasement?
In the implementation step of public education program development, you:

choices.text: determine the time and place for delivering the message.
determine the cost and benefits of the new program.

questions.question: Which step in the budget process occurs first?
Unsafe acts are:

choices.text: internal conditions that only apply to emergency situations.
external factors that should be recognized.
internal factors that the officer or crew performs.
external factors that should be corrected.

questions.correctChoiceText: internal factors that the officer or crew performs.

questions.question: Pre-incident planning priorities should first be directed toward items:
Capital budgets include:

choices.text: items such as EMS supplies and personal protective equipment.
items expected to last less than one year.
items expected to last three years or longer.

questions.correctChoiceText: items expected to last three years or longer.

questions.question: The primary fuel load for Type I construction is:
The ___ is responsible for monitoring hazardous conditions and recommending appropriate action at the incident scene.

Directions: Read the following statements, then select the correct answer from alternatives A-D below. Statement 1: If a new policy is issued, and you do not agree with it, you should not implement it. Statement 2: When a new policy is issued, you should have a meeting with your firefighter to introduce the new policy and answer any questions. Statement 3: If a new policy is issued and you have a question about it, you should ask for additional information prior to implementing it.

choices.text: Statements 1 and 2 are true; statement 3 is false.
Statement 1 is true; statements 2 and 3 are false.
Statement 1 is false; statements 2 and 3 are true.

questions.correctChoiceText: Statement 1 is false; statements 2 and 3 are true.

questions.question: Which function serves as the foundation of the management process?
Which statement is true with regard to these types of authority?

choices.text: In a decentralized department, the chief is not accountable for decisions.
Decentralization of authority allows for expeditious handling of most matters.
Centralized authority works well in very large organizations.
Decentralized authority grants authority at only one level.

questions.correctChoiceText: Decentralization of authority allows for expeditious handling of most matters.

questions.question: Line-item accounting is a form of:
The narrative portion of an accident report on an accident investigation should include:

choices.text: a copy of disciplinary action taken.

questions.question: A justification type narrative report may be written for a:
In verbal communications, interference such as anger, or an emotional state, can cause:

choices.text: undue stress on the sender only.
a better understanding of the message.
better feedback on the receiver's part.
